title: Israeli drones strike Lebanon despite US-brokered framework deal
summary: The latest strikes reportedly injure two, as Amnesty urges probe into earlier Israeli attacks which killed 24 civilians.
imageAlt: Israeli drones strike Lebanon despite US-brokered framework deal
aiSummary: The latest strikes reportedly injure two, as Amnesty urges probe into earlier Israeli attacks which killed 24 civilians.
keyPoints: Israeli drones strike Lebanon despite US-brokered framework deal
whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de internacional.

multimedia.alt: Israeli drones strike Lebanon despite US-brokered framework deal
multimedia.caption: Israeli drones strike Lebanon despite US-brokered framework deal

fullArticle:
Two people have been injured in an Israeli drone strike on a pick-up truck in southern Lebanon, according to state-run media, the latest attack despite a United States-brokered framework agreement intended to pave the way for a phased Israeli withdrawal.

The drone struck the vehicle as it was unloading garbage on the outskirts of the towns of Choukine and Kfar Dajjal in the Nabatieh district early on Friday, Lebanon’s National News Agency (NNA) said.

Separate strikes also targeted a car in southern Lebanon and demolition operations shook the border town of Khiam overnight.

Later, NNA reported drones also targeted the towns of Kfar Reman and Nabatieh al-Fawqa. No casualties were reported.
